acceptance: Button toggles state optimistically. Anonymous tap surfaces login modal. After add, button shows active state and persists across page reloads.
description: Per plan §7 push subsection. Add a star/bell button to the why-panel and the per-reach view on /path/now and the ranking page. Tap → if anonymous, prompt login; if authed, POST /watchlist. Authed users see active state when reach is already watched.
title: UI: 'Watch this reach' add-button on per-reach detail surfaces

acceptance: Chip row appears on multi-reach watersheds only. Selecting a chip changes the visible TQS and why-panel without page reload. Default selection respects ranking-page handoff and localStorage.
description: Per plan §7. When a watershed has ≥2 active reaches, render a chip row (Upper · Middle · Lower …) below the pill. Selecting a chip updates the pill, the why-panel, and any reach-aware sub-cards. Default reach: best-scoring when arrived from ranking page (state passed via location.state), else last-viewed from localStorage, else best-scoring. Single-reach watersheds hide the chip row.
title: UI: Reach selector chips + per-reach detail on /path/now

acceptance: Migration file exists in alembic/versions/. `alembic upgrade head` creates user_personas_catalog table with primary key 'key' and the 6 standard columns from plan §3. ALTER TABLE adds personas (default '{}'), personas_set_at (nullable), personas_version (default 1) to users. GIN index on users.personas. `alembic downgrade -1` cleanly reverses all changes. pytest test verifies columns exist after upgrade and are gone after downgrade.
description:
<context-digest>
Per plan §3, create the persona data plane in Postgres. One new table
(user_personas_catalog) and three new columns on existing users table
(personas varchar[], personas_set_at timestamptz, personas_version
smallint). GIN index on personas array for fast ANY() queries.
</context-digest>
Add the migration in alembic/versions/ following existing naming and
upgrade()/downgrade() conventions. Include both CREATE TABLE and ALTER
TABLE statements; the downgrade should DROP COLUMN and DROP TABLE.
notes: Migration h9c0d1e2f3a4_persona_self_selection.py committed as a663360. SQL output verified offline via 'alembic upgrade head --sql' — clean CREATE TABLE + 3 ALTER TABLEs + GIN index. Downgrade SQL verified via 'alembic downgrade h9c0d1e2f3a4:g8b9c0d1e2f3 --sql'. pytest test_persona_migration.py added; asserts information_schema state when run against a migrated DB. Local DB pre-existing stale state (alembic_version behind by ~6 revisions, schema partially applied outside alembic) prevents local upgrade head; not introduced by this migration. Production deploy applies it via riversignal-migrate Cloud Run Job.
title: Alembic migration: user_personas_catalog + personas columns on users

acceptance: Unit test with seeded history triggers one band-cross alert and one trend-rising alert. Re-running within 1h is a no-op. mute_until in the future blocks delivery.
description: Per plan §3.6. New job pipeline/alerts/compute_alerts.py runs hourly. For each user_reach_watches row: band-cross (current tqs ≥ threshold AND yesterday's snapshot < threshold) → fire band_cross_up. Slope over last 5 daily snapshots ≥ +4 tqs/day for a future target → fire trend_rising (rate-limited 5d/reach). Skip if muted_until > now. Writes user_alert_deliveries rows.
title: Alert engine: hourly cron computing band-cross + rising-trend deliveries

acceptance: GET with sample lat/lon + max_miles=150 returns sorted list; each entry has miles_from_user numeric. max_miles=0 returns empty array. Endpoint hits the watershed-rollup MV (one query).
description: Per plan §6. Ranking endpoint returns watersheds sorted by best-reach tqs DESC, with miles_from_user (great-circle from user point to best reach centroid), reach_spread, primary_factor, horizon_days. max_miles filter applies on distance.
title: API: GET /trip-quality/ranking?user_lat=&user_lon=&max_miles= with distance filter

acceptance: Badge appears when unseen ≥ 1, disappears when user marks all seen. Anonymous users see no badge.
description: Per plan §7. Bottom-nav exposes the alerts entry (either repurposed icon or new tab — decide post Phase A.5). Badge shows unseen notification count from GET /alerts?seen=false (polled or SWR-revalidated). Badge hides when 0.
title: UI: alerts badge on bottom-nav (unseen notification count)

acceptance: Pill renders on /path/now/<watershed>. Closed states render the closure badge. reach_spread ≥ 0.5 surfaces the affordance text. Type-check passes.
description: Per plan §7. Add a pill above existing hero metrics on RiverNowPage showing 'Trip Quality: 82 · Strong · Lower stretch' + reach_spread caveat ('Upper only — 2 of 3 reaches unfavorable') when reach_spread ≥ 0.5. When is_hard_closed on best reach, show clear closed badge instead of numeric score. Soft band copy from plan §2 score-bands table.
title: UI: Trip Quality pill on /path/now (watershed-level + best reach label)

acceptance: Running compute_trip_quality_daily on a clean DB produces one row per (reach_id, target_date) for the date range. is_hard_closed=true forces tqs into 0-29 band. primary_factor names a sub-score key. Unit test on a synthetic reach validates each sub-score function's edges.
description: Per plan §3.5. Implement compute_trip_quality_daily(start_date, end_date) writing to a staging table. v0.5 sub-scores: catch (from gold.predictions), water_temp (piecewise per §4.5, hard floors 70°F/40°F, warm-water shifted band), flow (piecewise from silver.flow_quality_bands), hatch (gold.hatch_chart × degree-day alignment), access (penalty-based + is_hard_closed gate). SKIP weather sub-score in this phase (weight redistributed equally across other 5: 0.20 each). Apply seasonal modifier from silver.tqs_seasonal_modifiers. Compute primary_factor = argmax(w_i × (100 − sub_score_i)). Confidence = max(20, 100 - horizon_days × 1.7).
title: Compute: pipeline/predictions/trip_quality.py (5 sub-scores, skip weather)

acceptance: Both tables exist with FKs cascading on user delete; UNIQUE(user_id, reach_id) on watches prevents dupes; idx_uad_user_unseen partial index for unseen notifications.
description: Per plan §3.0b. Create user_reach_watches (id, user_id FK, reach_id FK, alert_threshold int default 70, alert_trend bool default true, muted_until, created_at, UNIQUE(user_id, reach_id)). Create user_alert_deliveries (id, user_id FK, reach_id, alert_type, target_date, tqs_at_alert, channel, delivered_at, seen_at). Indexes per plan.
title: Migration: user_reach_watches + user_alert_deliveries tables

acceptance: All four verbs covered by pytest. UNIQUE(user_id, reach_id) violation on duplicate POST → 409. Threshold validation 0-100. muted_until parses ISO timestamp.
description: Per plan §6 push. Auth-required. GET returns current user's watched reaches enriched with current_tqs + trend_7d. POST adds a watch with optional alert_threshold (default 70) and alert_trend (default true). PATCH updates threshold/trend/muted_until. DELETE removes a watch. 401 when unauthenticated.
title: API: watchlist CRUD (GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/watchlist)

acceptance: Job runs to completion locally on a seeded DB. Second run on same day appends 0 new rows to history (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING via PK). Cron entry added to terraform/cloud_run_jobs.tf.
description: Add a Cloud Run job (or extend an existing daily job) that runs in order: REFRESH gold.trip_quality_daily CONCURRENTLY, REFRESH gold.trip_quality_watershed_daily CONCURRENTLY, INSERT today's reach×target_date snapshot rows into gold.trip_quality_history. Idempotent on re-run within same day.
title: Daily job: refresh trip_quality_daily + watershed rollup + history snapshot

acceptance: After alembic upgrade head, silver.river_reaches contains at least 20 rows spanning all 7 supported watersheds; mckenzie has Upper/Middle/Lower; deschutes has 4 reaches per plan. Each row has watershed, name, centroid_lat, centroid_lon set.
description: Per plan §3.0 seed table. Insert ~25 reaches across mckenzie/deschutes/metolius/johnday/klamath/skagit/green_river using the names from the plan. Populate centroid_lat/lon, primary_usgs_site_id (best-known gauge per reach), general_flow_bearing where determinable (NULL when sinuous), typical_species, is_warm_water. Add notes field 'needs_guide_review=true' for rows whose flow_bearing or primary gauge is provisional. Idempotent (ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING).
title: Seed: ~25 river_reaches across 7 watersheds (v0 draft, needs_guide_review)

acceptance: Alignment review complete; all gaps classified; execution issues created for real gaps; traceability matrix produced
description: Top-down reconciliation of RiverPath MVP: FEAT-012/014/015/016, US-040-058, plan-2026-04-12. Focus: what was built but not specced, what's specced but not built, what diverged.
notes:
<measure-results>
COMPLETENESS: 19 areas classified (2 ALIGNED, 2 INCOMPLETE, 3 DIVERGENT, 12 UNDERSPECIFIED)
TRACEABILITY: Matrix covers all 14 vision-to-code paths
ISSUE_COVERAGE: 8 execution beads created covering all non-ALIGNED gaps
ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA: 18/19 user stories SATISFIED, 1 UNTESTED (US-049 reading mode)
CONCERN_DRIFT: No active concerns declared
</measure-results>
title: align: RiverPath Mobile Web MVP alignment review

acceptance: Page loads, geolocate prompt works, ranking list renders sorted desc by score, distance filter applies, expand-on-tap shows per-reach scores. Bottom-nav stays visible.
description: Per plan §7. New route /path/where. Inputs: home location (zip or geolocate) and max miles (default 150). Renders ranked watershed list with best-reach name + score + drive distance (great-circle approximation v1) + primary factor + small reach_spread indicator. Tap row → expands to show all reaches in that watershed with their scores. Filter chip 'Only reaches ≥ 70' hides marginal.
title: UI: /path/where ranking page with location + distance filter

acceptance: Table exists with three seeded rows after upgrade head. Sum of deltas in any one modifier is exactly 0.0.
description: Per plan §2.7. Create silver.tqs_seasonal_modifiers (id, season_label, month_start, month_end, applies_to_species varchar[] NULL = all, w_catch_delta, w_water_temp_delta, w_flow_delta, w_weather_delta, w_hatch_delta, w_access_delta). Seed: dry-fly summer (Jun–Sep): hatch+0.05, catch-0.05. Winter steelhead (Dec–Feb, steelhead only): catch+0.10, hatch-0.10. Spring runoff (Apr–May): flow+0.05, catch-0.05.
title: Migration + seed: silver.tqs_seasonal_modifiers

acceptance: Oregon geologic polygons loaded into geologic_units table; unit_name, formation, rock_type, lithology, age fields populated; 95%+ Oregon land area coverage
description: Ingest USGS National Geologic Map Database geologic unit polygons for Oregon via ArcGIS REST service. Fields: unit_name, formation, rock_type, lithology, age_min_ma, age_max_ma, period, description, geometry. Design ref: W-030.
title: USGS NGMDB geologic unit ingestion adapter

acceptance: Table exists; user_id is nullable so anonymous feedback survives user deletion; CHECK constraint enforces rating in 1..5.
description: Per plan §3.0c. Create user_trip_feedback (id, user_id FK ON DELETE SET NULL, reach_id FK, trip_date, tqs_at_view, rating smallint 1-5, notes, submitted_at). Index on (reach_id, trip_date).
title: Migration: user_trip_feedback table

acceptance: Job runs to completion in <8 hours for all 7 watersheds × 10 years. bronze.weather_observations has ≥3000 rows per watershed after run. Re-run is no-op (UNIQUE constraint).
description: Per plan §5 Phase C. One-shot Cloud Run job pulls NOAA NCEI Climate Data Online daily summaries for each watershed (closest GHCN-Daily station) for the past 10 years. Chunked by year, throttled to NCEI rate limits, upserts bronze.weather_observations(source_type='ncei').
title: Backfill: NCEI CDO 10-year historical weather into bronze.weather_observations

acceptance: Migration creates the table with composite UNIQUE constraint; index supports descending-date scans.
description: Per plan §3.1. Create bronze.weather_observations with watershed, date, temp_max/min/avg_f, precip_in, wind_speed_avg, wind_gust_max, RH, cloud cover, snow_depth, pressure, source_type, source_station_id, data_payload jsonb, fetched_at, UNIQUE(watershed, date, source_type). Index on (watershed, date DESC).
title: Migration: bronze.weather_observations + watershed/date index

acceptance: GET /api/v1/reaches?watershed=mckenzie returns ≥2 reaches in JSON. No ?watershed → returns all active reaches. Returns 200 to anonymous callers.
description: Per plan §6. New router app/routers/reaches.py. GET /api/v1/reaches returns all active reaches (optional ?watershed= filter). Fields per plan §6 pull section.
title: API: GET /api/v1/reaches?watershed=… listing endpoint

acceptance: PRD updated with FEAT-014/015/016 references; RiverPath vision gaps table updated; B2C design plan supersession noted; downstream build beads created for Phase 1A foundation work
description: Thread RiverPath Mobile Web MVP wireframe requirements through artifact stack. Source: riverpath_mobile_web_mvp_features_v2.md, riverpath_wireframe_screen_map.md. New specs: FEAT-014 (mobile nav), FEAT-015 (explore/recreation), FEAT-016 (saved). Updated: FEAT-012 (41 FRs), user stories (US-046-058), parking lot (5 descoped items). Design plan: plan-2026-04-12-riverpath-mobile-mvp.md.
notes:
<measure-results>
ARTIFACT_CONSISTENCY: All updated artifacts consistent with authority order (Vision -> PRD -> Feature Specs -> Design Plans)
CONFLICT_RESOLUTION: No conflicts detected; PRD P2-7 (Mobile PWA) elevated to P1-10 through P1-16 with wireframe specifics
ISSUE_COMPLETENESS: 11 build beads created covering all phases (4 foundation + 7 feature screens)
DEPENDENCY_WIRING: 10 dependency edges wired; F1 (bottom nav) is critical path for all page shells
</measure-results>
title: evolve: RiverPath Mobile Web MVP wireframe-driven architecture

acceptance: Oregon land ownership polygons loaded; agency and collecting_status derived correctly; BLM=permitted, NPS=prohibited, USFS=restricted
description: Ingest BLM Surface Management Agency land ownership polygons for Oregon via ArcGIS REST service. Fields: agency, designation, admin_unit, collecting_status (derived from agency rules), geometry. Design ref: W-034.
title: BLM SMA land ownership ingestion adapter

acceptance: Page renders all three tabs. Adding/removing watches updates list without reload. Marking notifications seen calls API. Authed user with no data sees friendly empty-state on each tab.
description: Per plan §7. New route /path/alerts (auth-gated; anonymous → login modal). Three tabs: Watchlist (rows: reach + current tqs + 7d sparkline + threshold + mute toggle + remove), Notifications (chronological delivery cards with narrative + 'Plan this trip' deep-link), Digest (latest weekly digest with 14-day plots).
title: UI: /path/alerts page with Watchlist + Notifications + Digest tabs

acceptance: Pill and why-panel show '± N' when horizon ≥ 2 days. Today's view does not show the band. Number matches max(20, 100 - horizon*1.7).
description: Per plan §2 confidence subsection + §7. Display the confidence as a small '± N' next to the TQS pill and in the why-panel. Render at horizons where confidence < 90 (today is implicit; far-horizon is the value of the affordance).
title: UI: confidence band display on TQS surfaces (78 ± 12)

acceptance: Migration creates the table; horizon_days computed automatically on insert. UNIQUE constraint blocks duplicate inserts.
description: Per plan §3.2. Create bronze.weather_forecasts with watershed, issued_date, target_date, horizon_days GENERATED ALWAYS AS (target_date - issued_date) STORED, temp_max/min, precip_in, wind_speed_avg, cloud_cover, data_payload jsonb, fetched_at, UNIQUE(watershed, issued_date, target_date). Index on (watershed, target_date DESC).
title: Migration: bronze.weather_forecasts with horizon_days generated column

acceptance: FEAT-012 reflects all implemented features; no UNDERSPECIFIED gaps remain
description: FEAT-012 diverged significantly from implementation. Add FRs for: NWS weather forecast on River Now, USGS real-time gauge LIVE badge, snowpack card with fishing insights, species map page (/path/map), explore map page, Deep Time cross-sell card, Living River cross-sell on DeepTrail, Fahrenheit conversion, watershed header persistence.
title: Update FEAT-012 with weather, live gauges, snowpack, species map, cross-product cards

acceptance: Job runs idempotent for the week. Digest endpoint returns 200 + 14-day rollups per watched reach. Empty for users with no watchlist.
description: Per plan §3.6 + §6. Friday morning batch produces one digest per user with watched reaches. Each watershed_summary lists 14 days of TQS + band-crossings + ADR-007 narrative for the most-changed reach. Stored in user_alert_deliveries with alert_type='weekly_digest'. GET /api/v1/digest/weekly returns the most recent digest for the authed user.
title: Weekly digest job + GET /api/v1/digest/weekly

acceptance: POST with valid keys writes the array and sets personas_set_at. POST with empty array {personas: []} sets personas to '{}' and personas_set_at to now() (the 'skip' case). POST with an unknown or inactive key returns 400 with explanatory error. POST without auth cookie returns 401. pytest covers all four cases.
description:
<context-digest>
Per plan §5 + Validation Rules. POST with body { personas: [...] }.
Validate that every key in the request exists in user_personas_catalog
with is_active=true; unknown keys reject the whole request with 400.
Empty array is valid (skip case). Successful write: update users.personas
to the array, set personas_set_at = now(). Return updated payload.
</context-digest>
title: API: POST /api/v1/auth/personas with catalog validation

acceptance: API returns mineral deposits within radius; DeepTrail shows mineral cards with commodity, status, distance
description: Add /minerals/near/{lat}/{lon} API endpoint and mineral deposit cards to DeepTrailPage. FEAT-013 FR-14-16. Design ref: W-062.
title: DeepTrail: mineral sites section + API endpoint

acceptance: All endpoints return correct data; LLM tools registered and callable; geology lookup <500ms; fossil search <2s
description: Build 12 new REST endpoints (geology, fossils, land ownership, deep time) and 5 new LLM tool functions. Design ref: W-039 through W-044.
title: Geology REST API endpoints + LLM tools

acceptance: 5 angler test sessions completed. Findings written to docs/helix/04-test/tqs-hallway-test-*.md. Any blocking issues filed as new beads. Manual close.
description: Per plan §5 Phase A.5. Clickable prototype of pull surface (pill, reach chips, why-panel, ranking page) demoed to 5 anglers in person. Capture friction; iterate band copy, reach naming, info hierarchy. Document findings in docs/helix/04-test/tqs-hallway-test-2026-MM-DD.md. THIS IS A MANUAL GATE — does not auto-resolve. Phase B beads should not be claimed until this is closed.
title: GATE: Hallway test with 5 anglers before Phase B code ships

acceptance: MapLibre map renders on Fish+Refuge page; stations color-coded by thermal class; clicking station shows popup
description: FEAT-012 FR-26. FishRefugePage has thermal station grid but no map. Add MapLibre map with colored circle markers by thermal classification (blue/teal/amber/red).
title: Add cold-water refuge MapLibre overlay to Fish+Refuge

acceptance: Running the job inserts one row per watershed for yesterday. Re-running same day is a no-op (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING). Job has unit-level test covering parse + roll-up logic with sample NWS payload.
description: Per plan §3.1. New job pipeline/weather/nws_observations.py: for each watershed, fetch nearest NWS station's previous-day observations, roll up to daily totals/avgs, upsert one row keyed on (watershed, date, source_type='nws'). Wire into pipeline-daily chain or its own Cloud Run job.
title: Pipeline: daily NWS observations ingest into bronze.weather_observations

acceptance: alembic migration adds silver.river_reaches with all columns and indexes per §3.0. alembic upgrade head succeeds on a clean DB; alembic downgrade -1 cleanly removes the table.
description: Per plan §3.0. Create silver.river_reaches with id PK, watershed, name, short_label, river_mile range, bbox geometry, centroid lat/lon, primary_usgs_site_id, primary_snotel_station_id, general_flow_bearing, is_warm_water flag, typical_species varchar[], notes, source, is_active, timestamps. Indexes on watershed and bbox (GIST).
title: Migration: silver.river_reaches table + GIST/btree indexes

acceptance: Unit tests cover each penalty curve at boundary values. weather_score returns 100 for 60°F, no precip, wind 5mph, downstream. Returns ≤30 for 95°F, heavy rain, 20mph upstream, thunderstorm flag.
description: Per plan §4. Extend trip_quality.py: weather_score = 100 − temp_penalty − precip_penalty − wind_speed_penalty − wind_direction_penalty − thunderstorm_penalty. Use NWS forecast from bronze.weather_forecasts when horizon 2-7d; live obs when horizon 0-1d. Thresholds per plan §4.
title: Compute: full weather sub-score (temp/precip/wind/storm)

acceptance: Table created; UNIQUE constraint prevents same-day re-scrapes from duplicating rows.
description: Per plan §3.0d. Create bronze.guide_availability (id, guide_id, reach_id FK, target_date, availability_pct, source_url, fetched_at, UNIQUE(guide_id, reach_id, target_date, fetched_at::date)).
title: Migration: bronze.guide_availability table

acceptance: After dismissal, the modal does not re-appear in the same browser session even before /auth/me has been re-fetched. Across sessions, persistence relies entirely on personas_set_at from the API (no localStorage flag). Manual hallway test confirms no double-prompt.
description:
<context-digest>
Per plan §4 'When the prompt fires' and skip behavior. After the user
dismisses the modal (save or skip), record personas_set_at via the API.
That field stays set forever, so the prompt won't fire again. But during
the same session, if /auth/me hasn't yet reflected the write, also guard
client-side via a sessionStorage flag so we don't loop.
</context-digest>
title: Add session-scoped state to prevent persona prompt loop after skip

acceptance: Given site with intervention history, returns forecast with confidence scores; automated accuracy scoring when next monitoring data arrives
description: Prediction engine for species returns at intervention sites using gold.restoration_outcomes and LLM reasoning. Endpoint: POST /sites/{id}/forecast.
title: Build restoration forecast engine (FEAT-002)

acceptance: Migration creates silver.flow_quality_bands; PK enforces uniqueness; FK to silver.river_reaches.id cascades on delete.
description: Per plan §3.3. Create silver.flow_quality_bands with composite PK (reach_id, species, season). FK to silver.river_reaches. cfs_low, cfs_ideal_low, cfs_ideal_high, cfs_high, season_start_month, season_end_month, source.
title: Migration: silver.flow_quality_bands table

acceptance: Both response shapes match plan §6. is_hard_closed surfaced explicitly. partial_access_flag included. Anonymous-callable. Test covers both query forms + missing-params 400.
description: Per plan §6. /api/v1/trip-quality?date=…&reach_id=… returns the gold.trip_quality_daily row + the per-reach why-panel payload. /api/v1/trip-quality?date=…&watershed=… returns the watershed rollup + nested reaches array. 400 when neither reach_id nor watershed given.
title: API: GET /trip-quality reach + watershed forms

acceptance: GET /api/v1/personas/catalog returns 200 with a JSON array of exactly the 6 seeded personas in sort_order. Response shape matches plan §5 spec. Deactivating a row (is_active=false) excludes it from the response. pytest covers happy path + deactivation filter.
description:
<context-digest>
Per plan §5. Public read endpoint (no auth required). Returns active
personas from user_personas_catalog ordered by sort_order, mapped to
{ key, display_label, description, icon, sort_order }. Excludes rows
where is_active=false.
</context-digest>
title: API: GET /api/v1/personas/catalog returning active personas

acceptance: Reading mode toggle visible on story; Kids mode produces simpler vocabulary; Science mode includes citations and units; toggle persists on tab switch; LLM re-generates narrative on level change
description: FEAT-012 FR-12. Reading mode toggle (Kids/Adult/Science) on river story views. Kids: 5th-grade vocabulary, 'imagine you're standing in...' framing. Adult: default narrative. Science: technical with citations. LLM regenerates story at selected level. Toggle persists during session.
notes:
<measure-results>
PARTIAL: /sites/{ws}/story endpoint now accepts reading_level query param (kids/adult/science). Frontend toggle deferred to story page build — current story tab in SitePanel needs refactoring to use the param. Backend ready.
</measure-results>
title: Build reading mode toggle for river stories (FEAT-012 FR-12)

acceptance: Why-panel opens from pill tap. All six sub-scores visible with weight indicators. primary_factor row is visually emphasized. Closed states show the closure badge in place of a numeric tqs.
description: Per plan §2 + §7. Tap the TQS pill → modal/sheet showing six sub-scores (0–100 bars), 30-day sparkline of TQS from gold.trip_quality_history, primary_factor highlighted. Use plan's exact band copy. Partial-access badge surfaced when partial_access_flag=true.
title: UI: 'Why' panel with 6 sub-scores + weighted primary factor

acceptance: Settings UI shows current personas as chips. Edit opens the modal with checkboxes pre-checked for existing selections. Saving updates the row via POST /auth/personas and the chips reflect the new state. Type-check passes.
description:
<context-digest>
Per plan §4 Settings page section. Add a 'Your interests' card to the
existing user settings panel (currently in UserMenu dropdown or a
dedicated settings route). Shows current persona chips. 'Edit' button
opens PersonaPromptModal pre-populated with current selections. Saves
update users.personas + personas_set_at.
</context-digest>
title: Settings: 'Your interests' section with editable persona chips

acceptance: Sheet appears on first /path/now visit ≥1 day after a planned trip. Tap submits and dismisses. Dismiss-without-submit hides for 24h via localStorage.
description: Per plan §5 Phase B + §3.0c. After a user revisits /path/now/<watershed> following a planned trip date (or taps an alert), surface a low-friction sheet: '<Reach name> · <date>. How was it?' with 5 emoji buttons (1-5) and optional notes. Posts to /trip-feedback. Sheet hides after submit or explicit dismiss; dismissed state remembered for 24h.
title: UI: one-tap 'How was it?' feedback prompt on returned-from trips

acceptance: compute_trip_quality_daily produces sensible TQS for target dates 8-30 days out. Unit test verifies water_temp_score and weather_score draw from PRISM rather than NWS forecasts in this horizon. forecast_source column reads 'prism+trend'.
description: Per plan §3.5 source-layer table. For horizon 8-30 days, sub-scores use PRISM monthly normals (already in DB) blended with a 14-day recent-trend signal. Lower confidence per §confidence formula.
title: Compute: climatological fallback for 8-30d horizon (PRISM monthly + recent trend)

acceptance: HomePage shows species photo cards with common name, scientific name, photo; cards are scrollable; data from /sites/{ws}/species endpoint
description: Add scrollable species photo card section to HomePage using gold.species_gallery data. FEAT-012 FR-4. Design ref: plan-2026-04-11-b2c-products.md W-058.
title: RiverPath: species discovery section on home page

acceptance: On first load of a story card, reading level chip defaults to Kid for family_outdoor-only users and Adult for everyone else. User toggle override persists within a session. Type-check passes.
description:
<context-digest>
Per plan §6 Feature gating examples. The AI narrative reading level
toggle (Kid/Adult/Expert, already implemented for River Story) should
default to 'kid' when the user's personas include family_outdoor AND
do NOT include any of {guide_professional, angler_self_guided,
watershed_pro}. Otherwise default to 'adult'. User's per-session
override still takes precedence.
</context-digest>
title: Default AI narrative reading level to 'kid' for family_outdoor persona

acceptance: A fresh authed user with personas_set_at=null sees PersonaPromptModal after username setup completes. After dismissal, the modal does not appear again in the same session. A user with personas_set_at set never sees the prompt on subsequent sign-ins. Playwright (or manual hallway) test confirms the flow end-to-end.
description:
<context-digest>
Per plan §4 UX Flow diagram. After OAuth callback hits /auth/success,
the existing flow checks needsUsername; if true, redirect to username
setup. After username is set (or if it wasn't needed), show the
PersonaPromptModal once. Once dismissed (saved or skipped), proceed to
the intended landing page. Use the personas_set_at field on /auth/me
to decide whether to show the prompt: null = not yet asked.
</context-digest>
title: Wire PersonaPromptModal into post-signup flow (after username setup)

in the TQS plan; surface lives at /path/alerts when shipped) should only
appear in BottomNav for users whose personas include angler_self_guided
OR guide_professional. Users without a persona set (anonymous, skipped)
see it by default (we don't want to hide useful features from
un-tailored users), but users with a non-angler persona explicitly set
don't see it.

acceptance: All three plan phases (A, B, C) decomposed into individually buildable beads with spec-id, area labels, acceptance criteria, and dependency wiring. Convergence reached after ≤6 refinement rounds.
description:
<context-digest>
Decompose docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-persona-self-selection.md
into implementable beads. All 7 §9 decision points are resolved
(2026-05-13). Plan phases: A (DB+API ~2d), B (modal+settings ~3d),
C (feature gating ~2d). Six user-facing personas. Multi-select,
skippable, post-signup-after-username.
</context-digest>
notes:
<measure-results>
Decomposition: COMPLETE — 13 beads created spanning plan phases A (5), B (4), C (4).
All beads carry spec-id=02-design/plan-2026-05-13-persona-self-selection.md.
Convergence: round 1 made 13 beads + 17 deps; round 2 found 0 changes — converged immediately.
Concern threading: project tooling (alembic, pytest, FastAPI, TypeScript tsc) is correctly referenced in acceptance criteria. No vitest/jest mismatches.
Dependency integrity: no circular deps. Dep chains form a clean A → B → C order: A1 → A2 → A3/A5 → B1 → B2/B3 → B4; A4 → C1 → C2/C3/C4.
Sizing: each bead is one-cycle scope; no oversized items.
Label hygiene: every bead has helix + phase:build + kind:build + area:auth + secondary area (data/api/ui/ai).
</measure-results>
title: polish: decompose persona self-selection plan

acceptance: pytest runs; >80% coverage on business logic; integration tests verify cross-org isolation; LLM golden example suite from domain advisor

acceptance: GET /api/v1/auth/me for an authenticated user returns the existing payload plus personas (default '[]'), personas_set_at (null until set), and personas_version (1). Anonymous user response unchanged. pytest covers both authed and anonymous paths.
description:
<context-digest>
Per plan §5. The current /auth/me handler (app/routers/auth.py) reads
the JWT cookie and returns user info. Add three new fields to the
response: personas (array), personas_set_at (ISO timestamp or null),
personas_version (int). Anonymous response stays the same — these
fields are user-scoped.
</context-digest>
title: API: extend GET /api/v1/auth/me to include personas, personas_set_at, personas_version

acceptance: Design document converged with all 11 required sections; written to docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-10.md

notes:

SECTIONS_PRESENT: 11/11 (problem statement, requirements, architecture decisions, interface contracts, data model, error handling, security, test strategy, implementation plan, risk register, observability)
ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS: 8 decisions with alternatives evaluated (monolith, LLM reasoning, data storage, map framework, auth, PDF generation, background tasks, API design)
DEPENDENCY_GRAPH: Complete across all 6 P0 features with 28 work items
CONVERGENCE: Reached after 5 refinement rounds
CONCERN_MANDATED: No active concerns declared — no additional sections required
</measure-results>

acceptance: Design plan component and endpoint lists match codebase
description: plan-2026-04-12 missing: weather.py router, /snowpack, /conditions/live, /weather, SpeciesMapPage, ExploreMapPage, WatershedHeader, useWatershed, gold.snowpack_current, gold.aquatic_hatch_chart.
title: Update design plan with new endpoints and components

acceptance: Test cases: bearing 0°/45°/90°/180°/270° at 15mph each return expected penalties. NULL flow_bearing returns the bearing-less weather_score path.
description: Per plan §4 wind-direction subsection. delta = ((wind_bearing - reach.general_flow_bearing) + 360) mod 360. Penalty curve at wind ≥ 10 mph: 0±30° none, 90/270±30° −1×wind, 180±30° −2×wind, quartering −0.5×wind. NULL general_flow_bearing → skip the directional penalty (speed penalty still applies).
title: Compute: wind-direction penalty using reach.general_flow_bearing

acceptance: compute_trip_quality_daily output now includes weather_score column populated; tqs values shift accordingly. Existing tests updated. primary_factor now includes weather as a possible argmax.
description: Replace v0.5 5-sub-score average with full §2 weights (0.25 catch, 0.15 × 5 others). Pull weather from bronze.weather_forecasts (2-7d horizon) or live NWS observations (0-1d). Refresh gold.trip_quality_daily to include the weather contribution.
title: Wire weather sub-score into compute_trip_quality_daily + restore §2 weights

acceptance: Migration runs on top of A1 and inserts exactly 6 rows. Re-running is idempotent (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING). pytest test selects from user_personas_catalog and asserts the 6 expected keys are present with sort_order 10/20/30/40/50/60.
description:
